questions.question: 1. What specifically was the target of your annotation?

options.label: Data item, set, & series targets
options.tooltip: Annotation on data points, groups of items, or entire series

options.label: Coordinate space targets
options.tooltip: Annotation on values or ranges in the chart's coordinate system (min, max, average etc)

options.label: Chart element targets
options.tooltip: Annotation on structural parts of the chart (title, axes, legend, etc.)

options.label: Prior annotations
options.tooltip: Annotation referring to an existing annotation

questions.question: 2. What was your primary intent for this annotation?

options.label: Identify
options.tooltip: Indicate specific data points, elements, or areas of interest

options.label: Summarize
options.tooltip: Show key trends, statistics, or overall patterns

options.label: Compare
options.tooltip: Highlight differences or similarities between data elements

options.label: Present
options.tooltip: Provide additional context or explanations related to the data

options.label: Other
options.tooltip: Other

questions.question: 2-1. If you selected "Other," please describe your intent in detail.
3. On a scale of 1 to 5, how difficult was it to understand the caption when no annotation was provided?
3-1. Could you please explain the reason for your rating above?
1. What kind of data fact does your annotation imply?

options.label: Value/derived value
options.tooltip: A value of a data item or a calculated value

options.label: Distribution
options.tooltip: How values are spread across data

options.label: Difference
options.tooltip: A noticeable difference between data items

options.label: Extreme
options.tooltip: The highest or lowest value in a set

options.label: Rank
options.tooltip: The ordered position of a value

options.label: Categories
options.tooltip: Grouping data items into distinct categories based on their attributes

options.label: Cluster
options.tooltip: A group of similar data items

options.label: Outliers
options.tooltip: Data points that strongly differ from others

options.label: Association
options.tooltip: A relationship or correlation between variables or data elements

options.label: Trend
options.tooltip: A pattern of change over time or another dimension

options.label: Meta fact
options.tooltip: A fact about the data itself

options.label: Compound fact
options.tooltip: A combination of multiple facts

options.label: Other
options.tooltip: Other

questions.question: 1-1. If you selected "Other," please describe the specific data fact in detail.
2. On a scale of 1 to 5, how difficult was it to understand the caption when no annotation was provided?
2-1. Could you please explain the reason for your rating above?
